_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval.

procedure.name: Colonoscopy
procedure.indication: Active hematochezia in an anticoagulated patient (rivaroxaban) with orthostasis and anemia (Hgb 9.6); locate and treat lower GI bleeding source. Performed after ED stabilization, volume resuscitation, and coordinated hold of rivaroxaban with cardiology.
procedure.sedation: Monitored anesthesia care (propofol), anesthesia-directed given hemodynamic risk
procedure.prep_quality: Boston Bowel Prep Scale 6/9 — adequate (urgent inpatient prep; some residual liquid stool in right colon cleared with irrigation)
procedure.extent_reached: Cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve; terminal ileum intubated)

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy with endoscopic hemostasis. INDICATION: active hematochezia on rivaroxaban with orthostasis and anemia. Rivaroxaban held pre-procedure per cardiology; patient volume-resuscitated in ED with stabilized hemodynamics prior to scope. SEDATION: MAC/propofol, anesthesia-directed. PREP: adequate after irrigation (BBPS 6). FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum and the terminal ileum was intubated with no blood or lesion in the ileum, excluding a more proximal source. Moderate diverticulosis was present throughout the sigmoid and descending colon. In the proximal sigmoid, a single diverticulum demonstrated a non-bleeding visible vessel with adherent clot and surrounding fresh blood — identified as the culprit diverticular bleeding source. The clot was gently irrigated; hemostasis was achieved with placement of two through-the-scope hemoclips across the vessel, with no further bleeding after treatment. No mass, no ulcer, and no stigmata of active bleeding elsewhere. Non-bleeding internal hemorrhoids noted on retroflexion in the rectum. IMPRESSION: Diverticular hemorrhage from a proximal sigmoid diverticulum, treated successfully with hemoclips; diverticulosis; non-bleeding internal hemorrhoids. No malignancy identified.

findings.finding: Diverticular bleeding source — non-bleeding visible vessel with adherent clot and fresh blood
findings.action_taken: clot irrigation; endoscopic hemostasis with 2 through-the-scope hemoclips; hemostasis achieved

findings.finding: Moderate diverticulosis, no other stigmata of bleeding
Normal terminal ileum — no blood, excludes proximal source

disposition: Admitted for overnight observation and serial hemoglobin monitoring given anticoagulation and prior orthostasis. Hemodynamically stable post-procedure. Post-hemostasis and diverticular-bleed precautions reviewed with patient and daughter.

followup.recommendation: Serial hemoglobin/hematocrit monitoring during admission; transfuse per threshold and reassess for rebleeding
Coordinate timing of rivaroxaban resumption with cardiology, balancing nonvalvular afib stroke risk against rebleed risk after diverticular hemostasis
Watch for rebleeding after hemoclip hemostasis (recurrent hematochezia, drop in Hgb, orthostasis)
Outpatient GI follow-up to confirm resolution and set diverticular/colon surveillance plan

planted_followup_items.description: Diverticular hemorrhage in a patient on rivaroxaban with orthostasis and anemia (Hgb 9.6) — active lower GI bleed on a DOAC treated with hemoclips; hemodynamic and rebleed status must be actively followed
planted_followup_items.expected_action: admit for monitoring; serial Hgb, transfuse per threshold, escalate if rebleed

planted_followup_items.description: Post-hemostasis rebleeding risk from diverticular vessel after hemoclip placement
planted_followup_items.expected_action: monitor for recurrent hematochezia, falling Hgb, orthostasis; repeat colonoscopy or IR/angiography if rebleeds

planted_followup_items.description: Rivaroxaban was held peri-procedurally; resumption timing must be decided with cardiology given nonvalvular afib stroke risk vs. rebleed risk
planted_followup_items.expected_action: cardiology + GI joint decision and documented resumption plan before discharge

planted_followup_items.description: Cardiology coordination for anticoagulation management around the GI bleed
planted_followup_items.expected_action: contact cardiology for stroke-risk-informed anticoagulation plan

planted_followup_items.description: Outpatient GI follow-up to confirm bleed resolution and set colon surveillance interval (last full screening 2019)
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule GI follow-up and document surveillance plan

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat is a little scratchy and I feel gassy and crampy after all that air. Otherwise I'm just tired from being in the hospital.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-colonoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/cramping; no bleeding, no hemodynamic symptom
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; scratchy throat and gas/cramping are expected and self-limited within 24-48h. Encourage walking and hydration.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no bleeding or instability; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: The doctors stopped my blood thinner before the procedure. When am I supposed to start the rivaroxaban again? I'm nervous about my heart and a stroke.
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about anticoagulation resumption timing after held rivaroxaban; no symptoms reported
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the plan: rivaroxaban was intentionally held; do NOT restart on your own. GI and cardiology will set and document the resumption date balancing stroke risk against rebleed risk. Confirm she has that guidance in writing.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the follow-up plan; the correct message is to await the joint cardiology/GI resumption decision and not self-resume. Links to the med_resume item.

contacts.verbatim: I got so worried about my heart that I started taking my rivaroxaban again on my own three days ago. Now I'm seeing bright red blood in the toilet again and I felt woozy standing up this morning.
contacts.clinical_signal: Self-resumed anticoagulation too early after diverticular hemoclip hemostasis, now recurrent hematochezia with orthostatic symptom — likely rebleed unmasked by premature DOAC resumption
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for same-day/ED evaluation: hold rivaroxaban immediately, IV access, serial Hgb, type-and-screen, hemodynamic monitoring; anticipate repeat colonoscopy or IR angiography for rebleeding. Loop in cardiology re: anticoagulation.
contacts.rationale: Triage trap — a med-timing error (patient restarted anticoagulation before the sanctioned date) plus recurrent hematochezia and orthostasis is a red-flag rebleed on a DOAC requiring emergent action, not reassurance.
